description: Gate 20 (network-connect-non-public-ip): flip cbz→b in connect_policy.rs async fn connect(). The Rust async poll logic calls allow_local_binding().await?, then branches on the bool result: cbz w0, #.Lcontinue jumps past the error path when w0=0 (binding not allowed), falling through to build the PermissionDenied error with 'network target rejected by policy'. By replacing cbz with an unconditional branch (b #.Lcontinue), the error path is never reached — the connect always proceeds regardless of is_non_public_ip(addr.ip()). This lets codex's outbound CONNECT proxy reach localhost/RFC1918/link-local addresses without allow_local_binding=true in the config. Length-preserving: cbz (4 bytes) → b (4 bytes).

patches.description: cbz w0→b: skip the is_non_public_ip deny branch unconditionally
